findings.description: Docstring promises 'strictly YYYY-MM-DD' but strptime with %Y-%m-%d happily accepts non-zero-padded junk like '2024-1-1', so the strict contract is fiction.
clamp_to_range skipped the tz-awareness memo the rest of the module obeys: mixing naive/aware datetimes makes min/max raise a cryptic TypeError instead of this function's own ValueError contract.
The tz guard only checks tzinfo is not None, so a tzinfo whose utcoffset() returns None sails through and the subtraction blows up anyway — 'timezone-aware' validated in name only.
day_range materializes the entire date list, so business_days_between over a multi-year span allocates a small forest of date objects just to count them instead of doing arithmetic.
humanize_delta tops out at weeks, so a two-year-old timestamp proudly reports '104 w ago' — technically 'coarse', practically useless past a couple months.
is_weekend has no docstring while every one of its neighbors does — consistency apparently took the weekend off.
